zh.Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, B: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Carter於1621年出生在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特時，他的父親Thomas Carter11618歲，母親Joane Smith21歲。他於1647年8月4日與Agnes Ann Richards在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1677年2月28日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Carter于1621年出生在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特时，他的父亲Thomas Carter11618岁，母亲Joane Smith21岁。他于1647年8月4日与Agnes Ann Richards在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1677年2月28日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: First Recorded Slave in Portsmouth
historicEvents.description: In 1645, an African American man taken from Guinea was captured and brought to New Hampshire, he would be counted as the first African American slave in New Hampshire.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Black Heritage Trail in New Hampshire- Our African Heritage

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Carter was born in 1621, in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Carter, was 11618 and his mother, Joane Smith, was 21. He married Agnes Ann Richards on 4 August 1647, in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 28 February 1677, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Carter est né en 1621, à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Carter, avait 11618 ans et sa mère, Joane Smith, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Ann Richards le 4 août 1647, à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 28 février 1677, à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 56 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Carterが1621年にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターで生まれた時，父Thomas Carterは11618歳，母Joane Smithは21歳であった。1647年8月4日にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターでAgnes Ann Richardsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1677年2月28日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスで56歳で死去した。

ko.Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Carter(이)가 1621년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Carter(은)는 11618세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Smith(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1647년 8월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에서 Agnes Ann Richards(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1677년 2월 28일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, B: порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Carter родился в 1621 г. в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Carter было 11618 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Smith, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Ann Richards 4 августа 1647 г. в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 28 февраля 1677 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 56 лет.
